title: Tynsborough man charged with murder after human remains found buried under garage floor, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Shawn Sullivan, 40, of Tynsborough, charged with murder after human remains found under his garage floor.
The remains are believed to belong to Jill Kloppenburg, 47, missing from Lowell since January 2025.
Sullivan allegedly confessed to a friend he killed Kloppenburg; remains discovery followed radar garage search.

title: Why a Texas mom is running the Boston Marathon for her son — and his MGH doctor
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Adrienne Felterman is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise money for MGH and honor her son and his doctor.
Felterman's son Charles has Dup15q syndrome, leading her to seek top care from Dr. Ronald Thibert at Massachusetts General Hospital.
The family traveled from Houston to Boston for specialized care, emphasizing MGH's significant impact on Charles' quality of life.

title: ‘She is truly a superhero’: Why a Belmont athlete is running Boston for his wife
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Ben Flowers from Belmont is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for his wife, Coney, a cancer survivor.
Coney Flowers was diagnosed with Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia in 2024 and underwent over 70 days of treatment.
Ben Flowers raises funds for Mass General's Caring for a Cure, supporting families during medical treatments.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support kids with cancer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kathleen Labrie, 33, from Southampton, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Mass General's pediatric cancer team.
Labrie runs in memory of children who've battled cancer, adopting the motto 'I Run For Them' (IRFT).
Donors contributing to Labrie's run can honor someone with their donation, joining the IRFT community.

title: Noah Kahan announces premiere date for documentary coming to Netflix
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan's documentary 'Out of Body' will release on Netflix on April 13.
The documentary covers Kahan's life, Vermont roots, mental health, and success of 'Stick Season'.
Kahan's fourth studio album, 'The Great Divide', is set to release on April 24.

title: Jon Keller, longtime WBZ political analyst, returns to TV
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Jon Keller returns to WBZ-TV as a 'special contributor' after layoffs last fall.
Keller will offer political analysis and moderate debates; his show 'Keller At Large' resumes Sunday.
Keller was laid off after Paramount's sale to Skydance Media; he's an Emmy winner since 2005.

title: ‘A relic of a bygone era’: Here’s why readers want Massachusetts to stop changing its clocks
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers consider a bill to eliminate biannual clock changes and adopt Atlantic standard time permanently.
Senate President Karen E. Spilka supports legislation by State Sen. John F. Keenan pending coordination with other New England states.
A Boston.com poll shows 83% of over 470 readers favor ending the clock change, citing disrupted routines and health concerns.

title: Impaired Stoneham teen drove 118 mph, fled police, and then crashed, N.H. authorities say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old from Stoneham drove 118 mph and crashed while fleeing police in New Hampshire.
The teen failed to stop for Maine State Police before crashing into a wall in New Hampshire.
He faces charges, including felony reckless conduct and aggravated DUI, and is held at Rockingham County Jail.

title: The ‘World’s Shortest St. Paddy’s Day Parade’ is still New England’s nuttiest St. Pat’s fever dream
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: The 'World's Shortest St. Paddy's Day Parade' in Little Compton, Rhode Island measured 89 feet and drew 2,000 attendees.
The parade featured whimsical participants, including a woman dressed as a baked potato and a tractor pulling a dinosaur.
Filmmakers are exploring the parade's rivalry with Hot Springs, Arkansas, planning a documentary about the shortest parade title.

title: Massachusetts ER doctors say long wait times reflect a health system under strain
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts ranks third nationally for ER wait times, with patients averaging 189-minute stays, according to World Population Review.
Doctors attribute long ER waits to bed shortages, staffing issues, and an aging population causing healthcare system strain.
Emergency departments also handle gaps in primary care, as many patients lack access to regular doctors or faster treatment.
